data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ee0af7b8">
    <title>docprose: generate temporary Vale config</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx doc prose` must run with DDx defaults even when a project has no `.vale.ini`. DDx needs temporary Vale config generation so the public command stays `ddx doc prose` instead of making users manage Vale config.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:109-130 specifies temporary `.vale.ini` generation, packaged `StylesPath`, explicit paths, JSON output, and `--no-global`, but no config generator exists.

PROPOSED FIX
- Generate a temporary `.vale.ini` from DDx defaults and project config.
- Point `StylesPath` at packaged DDx Vale styles.
- Render project vocabulary into Vale vocab/config.
- Keep temp files scoped to the command invocation and clean them up.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not invoke Vale in this bead.
- Do not parse Vale JSON.
- Do not change user-facing findings.

DEPS
Depends on the style-pack skeleton because config generation needs packaged style paths.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestDocProseValeConfig_NoProjectValeINI` generates a valid config from DDx defaults.
2. `TestDocProseValeConfig_StylesPathUsesPackagedRules` verifies the generated `StylesPath`.
3. `TestDocProseValeConfig_ProjectVocabularyRendered` verifies vocabulary metadata is included.
4. `TestDocProseValeConfig_CleansTemporaryFiles` verifies cleanup behavior.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docprose, area:config, kind:implementation, prose-quality, adr:025, spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration, tier:standard</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" base-rev="1168d20e49dcaa6d073af56bb76e59d2c7cd1107" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T050136-7e552117"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" path="docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md">Plan: Prose Quality Integration with Vale</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs satisfied and committed. Here's the summary:

- **`cli/internal/docprose/valeconfig.go`** — `NewTempValeConfig(settings Settings)` creates a temp directory with a `.vale.ini` (StylesPath pointing at the temp styles dir, which symlinks the packaged DDx rules), and renders vocabulary accept/reject lists into `Vocab/Project/accept.txt` / `reject.txt`. `Cleanup()` removes the entire temp tree.
- **`cli/internal/docprose/valeconfig_test.go`** — the four required AC tests: `_NoProjectValeINI`, `_StylesPathUsesPackagedRules`, `_ProjectVocabularyRendered`, `_CleansTemporaryFiles` — all pass green. Full suite and lefthook both clean.
